diary: Monday started early—shower, quick protein shake, then hit the home gym before the real adventure began. Caught the bus to Capitol Hill feeling energized; brunch at Molly Moon's with the crew was exactly what I needed. The vibe was perfect, and when someone mentioned the USA-Wales match kicking off at 11, we all just looked at each other and knew. Kremwerk was packed but we grabbed a spot. The energy during the game was electric, even though the result was... well, not what we hoped for. Grabbed pizza at Serious Pie after to commiserate, then wandered through Pike Place—grabbed some fresh berries and just soaked in the Seattle sun. Headed home feeling pleasantly tired. Spent the evening gaming and watching shows, meal prepped for the week. It was one of those Mondays that felt less like work and more like living.
thought_process: I woke up with this restless Monday energy and decided to lean into it. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I was heading to Capitol Hill anyway, and honestly the sunny weather just pulled me toward the city instead of staying cooped up at home. The FIFA match timing felt serendipitous—I wasn't planning to watch it, but when the group texted about it, I figured why not? It gave the day structure and social momentum. I stayed in Capitol Hill longer than I expected because the weather was too nice to waste, then headed back to settle into a quieter evening. The day felt balanced—active morning, social midday, reflective evening. My mood shifted from that post-workout buzz to the match's competitive energy to a calmer, satisfied wind-down.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
